title: Aster diplostephioides
taxonomy: Aster diplostephioides is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, comprising over 23,000 species. The genus Aster is a group of flowering plants within the family Asteraceae, and Aster diplostephioides is a species within this genus. Aster diplostephioides is further classified as a member of the subfamily Asteroideae. It is a species of flowering plant native to China.

intro: Aster diplostephioides, also known as Western Aster or Mountain Aster, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits in relieving symptoms of fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aster diplostephioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Aster diplostephioides is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
